title: A systematic review of smart city governance in the Southern African Development Community
data_sources: policy documents and government reports (grey literature)

data_scope.study_type: qualitative systematic review of text (no geospatial datasets)
data_scope.region: Southern African Development Community (SADC, 16 member states)
data_scope.corpus: academic papers + policy documents/reports; exact included count not stated in public abstract/metadata
data_scope.time_window: not specified in public metadata; cited works ~2000s-2023

core_conclusion: A qualitative systematic review combining academic literature with policy documents finds smart-city scholarship and practice in SADC dominated by technology- and infrastructure-centric, Global-North framings, while regional urban dynamics, governance and digital-citizenship realities are under-examined. The literature is organized into five research themes, and the authors argue a credible SADC smart-city agenda must adopt local, governance-oriented lenses and confront digitalization and datafication rather than transplanting universal blueprints.
why_it_matters: Maps the knowledge gap for a rapidly urbanizing but under-studied region; treats policy/grey literature as evidence to capture practice not just discourse; reframes smartness as governance rather than gadgets; flags datafication as an equity and digital-divide risk.
why_nature: Corrects geographic imbalance in urban knowledge; couples digital infrastructure to society, citizenship and institutions; agenda-setting transferable five-theme synthesis; directly policy-translatable for SADC governments.
added_value_to_my_research: LLM/embedding literature-synthesis pipeline -> auditable FAIR evidence synthesis for telehealth/accessibility
Digital-divide + grid access surface -> hybrid physical+digital R2SFCA service catchments
Promise-realization gap index -> policy-to-outcome accountability metric for health access targets
Procedural-justice datafication audit -> fairness/bias guardrail for multi-source GeoAI fusion
SADC transferability stress test -> region-specific priors for transferable GeoAI / foundation-model fine-tuning
